issuesFound.title: Button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button contains only an aria-hidden span with a hamburger icon, providing no accessible name for screen reader users to identify the button's purpose.

issuesFound.explanation: The navigation toggle button controls the visibility of the nav-menu but lacks aria-expanded, preventing screen reader users from knowing whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.

issuesFound.title: title element empty

issuesFound.title: Landmark missing distinguishing label

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The first name input collects personal information but lacks an autocomplete attribute, preventing browsers and assistive technologies from automatically filling the field and reducing manual entry effort for users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: html element missing lang attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The html element does not have a lang attribute, preventing screen readers from announcing the page language correctly. This affects pronunciation and braille output for users who rely on assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible removes the outline without providing a visible alternative, failing to support high-contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces space between words and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: This utility class modifies the focus ring offset but does not restore a visible outline, leaving no focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons have a transition property that includes transform, causing spatial movement, but no prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform properties causing spatial movement, but the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query block is empty and does not disable the animation.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary button uses background #5588bb with white text, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 4.0:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text (13.5px font size).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1056: 
  background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .newsletter-section
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter section uses white text over a gradient background including #bb88ee (light purple), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.5:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all interactive elements that rely on this base rule, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 586: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. A correct implementation requires position: absolute and clipping to ensure content is visible only to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 613: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses text and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual impairments. Negative word-spacing is not recommended for body text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 676 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 676: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible replacement like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users will not see when a button is focused, hindering navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 793: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended touch target size. Even with padding, the total height may be insufficient for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 833: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px. With 10px padding, the total size is 40px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 847: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify when the mobile navigation toggle is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 913: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px with 0 padding. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it difficult to activate on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 999: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible state for the search button removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. Focus state is invisible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1009: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Links in the account navigation have text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover to indicate interactivity. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as color alone is not sufficient to distinguish links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1023: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transform: rotateY/rotateX with a transition. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1143 is empty and does not disable this spatial motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1143: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The focus-visible state for feature card links removes the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which card link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1193: .feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #aaaaaa on background #ffffff has a contrast ratio of 2.8:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text. This affects users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1233: .feature-card p { color: var(--colour-neutral-700); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tabs button focus state removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify the active filter button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1333: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is explicitly sized to 20px × 20px. This is below the 24px minimum and 44px recommended size, making it difficult to toggle for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1753: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox focus-visible state removes the outline without a visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the checkbox is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1838: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Multiple accessibility failures on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: This selector has three violations: (1) Focus indicator removed (outline: none); (2) Insufficient contrast (#94a3b8 on #555555 is 2.0:1); (3) Link underline removed with no non-color alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1773: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { color: #ffffff; text-decoration: underline; } Line 1773: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffffff; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Multiple accessibility failures on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: This selector has three violations: (1) Focus indicator removed (outline: none); (2) Insufficient contrast (#94a3b8 on #555555 is 2.0:1); (3) Link underline removed with no non-color alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1783: .social-links a { color: #ffffff; text-decoration: underline; } Line 1783: .social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffffff; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled open and closed via CSS classes, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never updated to reflect the expanded/collapsed state. Screen reader users cannot tell whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1858: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_open = true;`
    Line 1878: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown panels are shown and hidden, but the trigger button’s aria-expanded attribute is never synchronized with the visible state. Assistive technologies cannot announce the dropdown’s expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1955: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
    Line 1968: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set, so screen readers assume the menu state is unknown until first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1896: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_initialised = true;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox but does not toggle aria-expanded when the suggestion list opens and closes. Screen readers cannot announce the popup state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2453: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);` after `_searchState.open = true;`
    Line 2465: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Switching billing periods updates prices visually but does not announce the change or the new prices to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2838: After `emit(document, 'pricing:periodchange', ...);`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.announce.pricingMonthly || 'Prices switched to monthly billing' : STRINGS.announce.pricingAnnual || 'Prices switched to annual billing');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu triggers missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown triggers are registered, they are given aria-haspopup and aria-controls but not the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute required by the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1992: Add `setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);` after setting aria-controls

issuesFound.title: productFilter result changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge updates visually but no live-region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product list has changed or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3065: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce(_productFilterState.activeCategory === (CONFIG.products.allCategoryValue || 'all') ? STRINGS.announce.filterAll || 'Showing all products' : STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(_productFilterState.activeCategory));`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons indicate active category visually but do not expose the pressed state via ARIA, making it impossible for assistive technology users to know which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3105: Add `setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true". Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1203: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but leaves aria-invalid="true" (or doesn't explicitly set it to false), causing screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1223: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` (or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`) at the end of the function

issuesFound.title: mobileNav state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation menu is not announced to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1859: After `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`, add:
    `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 1879: After `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`, add:
    `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus is not announced, so screen reader users cannot tell when a submenu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1956: After `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true);`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');`
    Line 1969: After `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`, add:
    `if (returnFocus) { inst.trigger.focus(); } liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When a user removes a filter chip, the product list updates but the removal action and the resulting filter state are not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3218: After `render();`, add:
    `liveRegion.announce('Filter removed');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap declared but never activated
issuesFound.explanation: A focus trap variable is declared (`let _trap = null;`) but is never initialized or activated. When the mobile menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing keyboard users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1896: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` after `_initialised = true;`
    Line 1859: Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` in the open function
    Line 1879: Add `if (_trap) _trap.deactivate();` in the close function (before unlocking scroll)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown closes (especially via Escape key), the returnFocus parameter is accepted but never used to return focus to the trigger button, leaving focus lost or in an unexpected location.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1970: Add `if (returnFocus) inst.trigger.focus();` after setting aria-expanded to false

issuesFound.title: pricing switch missing aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle is assigned role="switch" but aria-checked is never updated to reflect whether the switch is "on" (annual) or "off" (monthly). Screen readers cannot convey the switch state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2856: After setting role="switch", add:
    `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(_pricingState.period === 'year'));`
    Line 2806: In setPeriod, after `_pricingState.period = period;`, add:
    `if (_toggleEl) setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, String(period === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox but does not initialize aria-expanded="false", so assistive technologies assume the popup is expanded by default.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2512: After `setAttr(input, 'aria-haspopup', 'listbox');`, add:
    `setAttr(input, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not update the aria-expanded state on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed when the menu state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1675: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1695: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init function initializes the navigation trigger but does not set the initial aria-expanded attribute to "false" on the _trigger element. Assistive technologies may announce the incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1745: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance and dropdownMenu._closeInstance functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the aria-expanded state on the inst.trigger element. Screen reader users will not be informed when the dropdown state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1835: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1850: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu.register function sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls on the trigger but does not set the initial aria-expanded attribute to "false". Assistive technologies may announce the incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1905: setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2780: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch._open and siteSearch._close functions toggle the visibility of the suggestion listbox but do not update the aria-expanded state on the _inputEl. Screen reader users will not be informed when the suggestion panel state changes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2335: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2345: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init does not set initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch.init function mounts the suggestion listbox and sets aria-controls on the input but does not set the initial aria-expanded attribute to "false". Assistive technologies may announce the incorrect initial state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2450: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce changes to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters function updates the visibility of product cards and the count badge dynamically but does not announce these changes to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT will not know the results have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2590: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod function updates price displays across all plan cards when the billing period changes but does not announce this update to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT will not know the prices have updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3010: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop onClick does not announce action to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollToTop._onClick function scrolls the page to the top but does not announce this action to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT will not be confirmed that the scroll action completed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2185: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');

fpTitles: dropdownMenu state changes not announced

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands and collapses mobile navigation sections has aria-controls but no aria-expanded attribute. Assistive technology cannot announce the current state of the section.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1150: <button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown decorative chevron not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The chevron icon inside the dropdown trigger is decorative but not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the triangle character as "black up-pointing triangle" or similar.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1076: {isOpen ? '▲' : '▼'}

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed

issuesFound.fix: Line 1545: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle annual button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The annual billing button shows selection state via className but lacks aria-pressed, so screen readers cannot announce whether annual billing is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The video play/pause button toggles state but only updates its aria-label. It lacks aria-pressed, which is required for toggle buttons to communicate their on/off state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1305: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list used for the seamless marquee animation is not marked with aria-hidden, causing screen readers to announce the integration partners twice.

issuesFound.title: SiteHeader mobile menu toggle missing aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid prop mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component accepts an invalid prop for styling but does not map it to aria-invalid, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state to users.

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email field broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references an error message ID 'newsletter-email-error' via aria-describedby, but FormGroup is not passed an errorId prop, so the error element is never rendered with that ID. This creates a broken reference when errors occur.

issuesFound.title: ContactForm message field broken aria-describedby reference

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm submit button broken aria-describedby and duplicate ID
issuesFound.explanation: The submit button references statusId 'newsletter-status' via aria-describedby, but this element only exists when status is success or error, not when idle. Additionally, both the success message and ErrorMessage use the same ID 'newsletter-status', creating a duplicate ID violation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1650: Remove aria-describedby from Button or ensure the target element always exists. Line 1680: Change ErrorMessage id to "newsletter-error". Line 1645: Change success p id to "newsletter-success".
